text: The surgeon initiates a low anterior resection by mobilizing the sigmoid colon from medial to lateral using monopolar electrocautery. The ureter is identified and preserved, with ICG technology confirming its identity.

The surgeon uses a sixty-five vascular load powered stapler to ligate the inferior mesenteric vein. The inferior mesenteric artery is then clipped twice proximally and once distally, followed by cauterization and cutting with a bipolar energy device at the base of the aorta.

The surgeon begins the total mesorectal excision, starting anteriorly between the rectum and the vagina, then proceeds posteriorly and laterally in the avascular plane. The dissection is carried down to the level of the levator muscles, with the rectum completely circumferentially dissected to this level.
The surgeon uses a blue load, sixty-five powered stapler to transect the rectum, requiring multiple firings to achieve complete transection and resection.

Indocyanine green (ICG) technology is used to assess perfusion before resecting the sigmoid colon. Following the resection, the surgeon performs an end-to-end colorectal anastomosis using a powered EEA stapler. An air leak test is conducted, showing no leaks.
The surgeon performs an ultrasound examination on both the left and right lobes of the liver, revealing the segment six metastatic lesion.
The surgeon performs a wedge resection of the metastatic lesion in the liver using a vessel sealer. Adequate hemostasis is achieved, followed by the ablation of the liver margin.
